title: Board Specific Roles
excerpt:

posts.author: shirozayas (Shiro Zayas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Hey everyone! I’m looking for a way to make board-level ownership a lot cleaner and more intuitive. Right now, if I want to show who the big players are for an entire project—like the Project Manager, Legal & Compliance lead, or the main Approver—I have to add a People Column and repeat that info on every single item. What I’m dreaming of is the ability to assign specific roles at the board level itself. Imagine being able to designate a “Project Owner” or “Compliance Lead” in the board settings once, and having that role carry through the whole board without the extra clutter of a column for every row. It would make it so much easier to see exactly who is responsible for the big picture at a single glance!

What are you trying to achieve

I’d love to see a dedicated “Board Roles” or “Project Stakeholders” section within the board header or settings where we can define custom roles—like Project Manager, Approver, or Legal & Compliance—and assign them to specific team members for the entire board. Instead of cluttering the grid with repetitive columns, these assignments would live at the board level, providing high-level visibility to anyone viewing the project. This would drastically improve project governance and clear up valuable screen real estate, making it immediately obvious who the key points of contact are without needing to scroll through individual tasks. Bonus: you could use this for automations as well.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Shiro!

We ended up doing something similar with teams + people columns + column/board restrictions. It works, but yeah… not elegant.

I like what you’re proposing, but I keep coming back to how monday would actually scale it.

Like is this just a “display” layer for who’s the PM, approver, etc., or would you expect it to actually drive permissions and automations too?

Because if it’s just visibility, that feels easy. If it starts tying into permissions, then you’re basically getting into per-role or per-user permission logic, which is a pretty big shift from how monday works today.

Also curious how you’d expect it to interact with board owners, teams, and existing permissions.

Conceptually I love it. Implementation-wise… feels like it could get complicated fast
